name: edit-phase-body-under-same-title
baseline:
# Notifications service

Stand up a service that fans out user notifications.

<Phase title="Define the event schema">
Agree on a JSON envelope for every notification event.
</Phase>

<Phase title="Wire the delivery workers">
Workers pull from the queue and call the email provider.
</Phase>
current:
# Notifications service

Stand up a service that fans out user notifications.

<Phase title="Define the event schema">
Agree on a versioned JSON envelope for every notification event, with a required type discriminator.
</Phase>

<Phase title="Wire the delivery workers">
Workers pull from the queue and call the email provider.
</Phase>

name: edit-h2-body-under-same-heading

current:

name: add-phase-in-middle
baseline:
# Data warehouse migration

Move reporting from the legacy MySQL replica to Snowflake.

<Phase title="Stand up Snowflake">
Create the account, warehouses, and role hierarchy.
</Phase>

<Phase title="Cut over dashboards">
Repoint the BI tool at the new warehouse.
</Phase>
current:
# Data warehouse migration

Move reporting from the legacy MySQL replica to Snowflake.

<Phase title="Stand up Snowflake">
Create the account, warehouses, and role hierarchy.
</Phase>

<Phase title="Backfill historical data">
Load the last two years of fact tables and validate row counts.
</Phase>

<Phase title="Cut over dashboards">
Repoint the BI tool at the new warehouse.
</Phase>

name: add-callout-and-checklist-at-end
baseline:
# Feature flag cleanup

Remove stale flags that have been fully rolled out.

<Phase title="Inventory flags">
List every flag and its current rollout percentage.
</Phase>
current:
# Feature flag cleanup

Remove stale flags that have been fully rolled out.

<Phase title="Inventory flags">
List every flag and its current rollout percentage.
</Phase>

<Callout type="risk">
Deleting a flag still referenced in code will break the build.
</Callout>

<Checklist title="Removal steps">
- Remove the flag check
- Delete the flag definition
- Drop the analytics event
</Checklist>

name: remove-callout-section
baseline:
# Password reset flow

Rebuild the forgotten-password experience.

<Phase title="Token generation">
Generate a single-use, time-boxed reset token.
</Phase>

<Callout type="warn">
Never log the reset token in plaintext.
</Callout>

<Phase title="Reset page">
Serve the form that consumes the token and sets a new password.
</Phase>

name: rename-phase-title-body-unchanged
baseline:
# Billing integration

Integrate the Stripe billing API.

<Phase title="Build the limiter">
Implement the per-customer rate limiter using a Redis sliding window and a fail-open fallback when Redis is unavailable.
</Phase>

<Phase title="Invoice sync">
Mirror Stripe invoices into our database on webhook receipt.
</Phase>
current:
# Billing integration

Integrate the Stripe billing API.

<Phase title="Build the rate limiter">
Implement the per-customer rate limiter using a Redis sliding window and a fail-open fallback when Redis is unavailable.
</Phase>

<Phase title="Invoice sync">
Mirror Stripe invoices into our database on webhook receipt.
</Phase>

name: rename-heading-body-mostly-unchanged
baseline:
# Incident runbook

## Detection

Alerts fire from the latency SLO burn-rate monitor and page the on-call engineer through PagerDuty within two minutes.

## Mitigation

Drain the unhealthy region and shift traffic to the standby.
current:
# Incident runbook

## Detect the incident

Alerts fire from the latency SLO burn-rate monitor and page the on-call engineer through PagerDuty within two minutes.

## Mitigation

Drain the unhealthy region and shift traffic to the standby.

name: rename-checklist-title-body-unchanged
baseline:
# Release process

Standardize how we cut a production release.

<Checklist title="Pre-flight">
- Freeze the release branch
- Run the full e2e suite
- Verify migrations are reversible
- Post the changelog to the channel
</Checklist>
current:
# Release process

Standardize how we cut a production release.

<Checklist title="Pre-flight checks">
- Freeze the release branch
- Run the full e2e suite
- Verify migrations are reversible
- Post the changelog to the channel
</Checklist>

name: rename-phase-title-borderline-half-rewrite
baseline:
# API gateway rollout

Put every public endpoint behind a managed gateway.

<Phase title="Pilot one service">
Route the read-only catalog service through the gateway and watch p99 latency for a week before expanding.
</Phase>

<Phase title="Document the contract">
Publish the OpenAPI spec for partners.
</Phase>
current:
# API gateway rollout

Put every public endpoint behind a managed gateway.

<Phase title="Initial limited rollout">
Route the read-only catalog service through the gateway first, enable request logging and per-route quotas, then review error budgets before onboarding the write services.
</Phase>

<Phase title="Document the contract">
Publish the OpenAPI spec for partners.
</Phase>

name: rename-heading-borderline-heavy-reword
baseline:
# Cache layer

## Eviction policy

Use an LRU policy with a fixed memory ceiling and a sixty second TTL on every entry to bound staleness.
current:
# Cache layer

## Memory management

Apply LRU eviction, cap the heap at a fixed ceiling, and expire keys after sixty seconds so reads never serve badly stale data while keeping the hit rate high.

name: edit-callout-body-titleless
baseline:
# Webhook hardening

Make inbound webhook handling resilient.

<Callout type="risk">
Unsigned webhook payloads can be spoofed by anyone who knows the URL.
</Callout>

<Phase title="Verify signatures">
Reject any request whose HMAC signature does not match.
</Phase>
current:
# Webhook hardening

Make inbound webhook handling resilient.

<Callout type="risk">
Unsigned webhook payloads can be spoofed by anyone who knows the URL, and replayed payloads can double-charge customers.
</Callout>

<Phase title="Verify signatures">
Reject any request whose HMAC signature does not match.
</Phase>

name: mixed-edit-add-remove
baseline:
# Customer export feature

Let customers download all their data on demand.

<Phase title="Gather data">
Query every table that holds customer-owned records.
</Phase>

<Callout type="note">
Exports must complete within the request timeout.
</Callout>

<Phase title="Package the archive">
Zip the JSON files and store them in a bucket.
</Phase>
current:
# Customer export feature

Let customers download all their data on demand.

<Phase title="Gather data">
Query every table that holds customer-owned records, including soft-deleted rows that the customer can still recover.
</Phase>

<Phase title="Package the archive">
Zip the JSON files and store them in a bucket.
</Phase>

<Phase title="Email the download link">
Send a signed, expiring URL once the archive is ready.
</Phase>

name: mixed-rename-edit-titleless-add
baseline:
# Fraud detection rollout

Deploy the real-time fraud scoring model.

<Phase title="Shadow mode">
Score live transactions without acting on the result and compare against the current rules engine.
</Phase>

<Compare>
Rules engine vs ML model on the holdout set.
</Compare>
current:
# Fraud detection rollout

Deploy the real-time fraud scoring model.

<Phase title="Run in shadow mode">
Score live transactions without acting on the result and compare against the current rules engine.
</Phase>

<Compare>
Rules engine vs ML model on the holdout set, broken down by transaction size band.
</Compare>

<Phase title="Enforce blocks">
Start declining transactions above the score threshold.
</Phase>

name: edit-questions-body-under-same-title
baseline:
# Pricing experiment

Test a usage-based pricing tier.

<Questions title="Open questions">
- What is the unit of metering?
- How do we handle overage?
</Questions>

<Phase title="Instrument usage">
Emit a metering event on every billable action.
</Phase>
current:
# Pricing experiment

Test a usage-based pricing tier.

<Questions title="Open questions">
- What is the unit of metering?
- How do we handle overage?
- Do existing customers get grandfathered onto the old plan?
</Questions>

<Phase title="Instrument usage">
Emit a metering event on every billable action.
</Phase>
